_meta.source: mortgagecalculator.org 'Mortgage Refinance Breakeven Calculator' article, 'Example A' worked example.
substitution_note: Bankrate's refi calculator (https://www.bankrate.com/mortgages/refinance-calculator/) and NerdWallet's (https://www.nerdwallet.com/mortgages/refinance-calculator) are both JS-rendered with no default-example output in the static HTML. We substituted with mortgagecalculator.org which publishes Example A and Example B inline.
Example A inputs: old $200,000 @ 5.000% with 240 months remaining; refi to 15-year (180mo) @ 3.250%; closing costs $6,000 (1 discount point + 1 origination point).
Engine matches old_pi ($1,319.91), new_pi ($1,405.34), and payment_change (+$85.43) to the cent.

Oracle text claims 'Months to Breakeven = 21' even though the monthly payment INCREASES. That definition is non-standard (it appears to be measuring something like 'months until cumulative lifetime-interest savings exceed closing costs', which is an interest-payoff breakeven, not a cashflow-savings breakeven). Engine reports simple_status='no_savings' for this scenario because monthly_savings < 0, which is the project's documented simple-breakeven definition. We assert the cashflow numbers (which agree) and document the 21-month claim as a different metric (NOT asserted).

expected.oracle_alternative_breakeven_21_months_n: interest-savings breakeven; different definition from simple-cashflow breakeven
